_comment: P33 dependency + MCP-server seed for source-registry.json. Load via: python3 tools/source_currency.py seed-sources canonical-sources/dependency-sources-seed.json. Version drift is checked token-free by tools/dependency_currency.py against PyPI/GitHub (upstream_api + check_url); source_currency.py owns registry writes. Each entry names what it protects (used_by) and why a bump matters (_why). MCP entries name the shared/connectors/connectors.json connector id(s) they back so drift invariant 23 can verify the loop.
name: MCP Python SDK (mcp)
extraction_hint: SDK release notes for transport/auth and tool-registration API changes tracked to the MCP spec
_why: the local MCP server (tools/mcp_server.py) is built on this SDK; a transport or tool-schema change in lockstep with the MCP spec breaks tool exposure

name: python-dateutil (dateutil)
extraction_hint: relativedelta / rrule API changes affecting recurrence and due-date math
_why: import-guarded in tools/tasks.py for recurrence and relative-date math; absent, task date math degrades to a stdlib approximation, so a bump is low-risk but tracked for the default install

name: NumPy (numpy)
extraction_hint: major dtype/API changes; NumPy 2.x ABI break vs opencv/moviepy wheels
_why: import-guarded in mediaprobe.py and pulled transitively by opencv/moviepy; pinning it explicitly keeps the videoedit stack ABI-consistent for the default install

name: sqlite-vec
extraction_hint: vec0 virtual-table / loadable-extension API changes affecting the semantic cache
_why: import-guarded in shared/cache/semantic.py for vector search; absent, the scoop cache falls back to lexical match, so a bump is low-risk but tracked for the default install

name: PyYAML (yaml)
extraction_hint: safe_load / loader API changes affecting SKILL.md frontmatter parsing in the drift guard
_why: import-guarded in the drift guard (tools/sync_check.py) for SKILL.md frontmatter; absent, it falls back to a regex parse, so a bump is low-risk but tracked for the default install

name: PySceneDetect (scenedetect)
extraction_hint: ContentDetector / scene_manager API changes; the get_seconds()->seconds rename in 0.7
_why: pinned >=0.7,<0.8; a 0.8/major bump renames API (get_seconds->seconds shim precedent) and breaks scene detection

name: MoviePy (moviepy)
extraction_hint: breaking renames; removal of moviepy.editor; a v3 major
_why: pinned >=2,<3; v2 was a breaking rewrite (renamed methods, removed moviepy.editor). A v3 would break the shorts render lane

name: OpenTimelineIO (opentimelineio)
extraction_hint: schema version bumps; adapter packaging (adapters split into separate pip packages since 0.17/0.18)
_why: the neutral timeline core round-trips through OTIO; a schema or adapter-packaging change breaks build/parse

name: OTIO FCPX XML adapter (otio-fcpx-xml-adapter)
extraction_hint: adapter API/compat with the installed opentimelineio core
_why: FCPXML round-trip depends on this adapter matching the OTIO core version

name: OTIO Kdenlive adapter (otio-kdenlive-adapter)
extraction_hint: early 0.0.x adapter; API is unstable, watch every release
_why: the MLT/Kdenlive read path uses this pre-1.0 adapter; any release can change the API

name: PyAV (av)
extraction_hint: libav/ffmpeg ABI compatibility; a major that drops an ffmpeg series
_why: windowed-RMS silence backend; PyAV tracks the ffmpeg ABI, so an ffmpeg bump can force a PyAV bump

name: jiwer (WER/MER/WIL accuracy decomposition)
extraction_hint: process_words/process_characters API; RapidFuzz backend changes
_why: optional accuracy-validation extra for local STT (P45); wer.py covers the stdlib floor, jiwer adds MER/WIL/WIP decomposition

name: OpenCV headless (opencv-python-headless)
extraction_hint: the backend PySceneDetect uses; a major that changes cv2 imports
_why: PySceneDetect ContentDetector runs on OpenCV; an incompatible major breaks scene detection

name: Playwright (playwright)
extraction_hint: bundled Chromium version and the sync API surface used by acquire.py
_why: JavaScript-render path for competitor snapshots; the Chromium bundle and API drift

name: Requests (requests)
extraction_hint: stable HTTP client; only a major (3.x) matters
_why: HTTP client under the fetch stack; low churn, long interval

name: charset-normalizer
extraction_hint: encoding detection used by the fetch stack; only a major matters
_why: charset detection for fetched pages; low churn

name: BeautifulSoup (beautifulsoup4)
extraction_hint: HTML parse API used by the competitor metadata parser; only a major matters
_why: parses competitor page HTML (SIGI_STATE, og tags); stable API, long interval

name: FFmpeg / ffprobe (system binary)
extraction_hint: silencedetect / scdet filter output format and flag names; a major that changes stderr shape
_why: core media backend; the committed silencedetect/scdet stderr fixtures are parsed by regex, so a filter/flag change breaks detection. Not pip-installed; check the release page manually

name: MLT framework / melt (system binary)
extraction_hint: MLT XML schema and melt CLI changes; new avformat/ffmpeg support
_why: Shotcut/Kdenlive render lane; the MLT XML we emit and the melt CLI we shell out to can drift

name: DaVinci Resolve Scripting API
extraction_hint: the in-install Developer/Scripting/README.txt; the supported Python interpreter range
_why: Lane B (live Resolve control) is Studio-only and pins Python 3.10 to 3.12; a Resolve major that moves the interpreter breaks the fusionscript bridge

name: Apple FCPXML reference / DTD
pinned_constraint: version read from the installed FCP DTD
extraction_hint: FCPXML version bumps (FCP 11 -> 1.13, 12.x -> ~1.14); DTD element changes
_why: fcpxml.py reads the target version from the on-disk DTD; an FCP major bumps the FCPXML version and can change validation

name: Apple Compressor CLI
extraction_hint: the Compressor -batchname/-jobpath CLI flags
_why: macOS batch-export lane; the CLI flag surface can change across Compressor versions

name: CommandPost (macOS automation)
extraction_hint: the Lua automation surface CommandPost exposes
_why: the escape hatch for FCP UI-driven steps; its automation API can change

name: Google Workspace MCP (workspace-mcp)
extraction_hint: tool names, OAuth 2.1 scopes, and tool-tier flags used by the Google connectors and the wizard
_why: the actual server backing every Google Workspace connector; a tool-name or auth change breaks Gmail/Calendar/Drive setup

name: Microsoft 365 MCP (Softeria ms-365-mcp-server)
extraction_hint: device-code auth flow and tool names for Outlook/Calendar/Excel/OneDrive
_why: the server backing every Microsoft 365 connector; auth-flow or tool-name drift breaks Outlook/Excel/OneDrive setup

name: Wolfram Alpha MCP (mcp-wolfram-alpha)
extraction_hint: tool names and the App ID auth; release page slug pending confirmation
_why: exact symbolic computation lane; a tool-name change silently drops it to guidance-only

name: E2B Code Interpreter MCP
extraction_hint: sandbox API and tool names; release page slug pending confirmation
_why: sandboxed Python for statistical analysis; an API change drops the compute lane to guidance-only

name: stats-compass-mcp
extraction_hint: the 50+ pandas tool names for t-tests/ANOVA/chi-square
_why: the pandas hypothesis-testing lane; a tool-name change drops it to guidance-only

name: DuckDB / MotherDuck MCP (mcp-server-motherduck)
extraction_hint: SQL tool names and local vs MotherDuck mode
_why: analytical SQL over CSV/Parquet/JSON; a tool-name change drops the data-query lane

name: Jupyter MCP (jupyter-mcp-server)
extraction_hint: persistent-notebook tool names; release API pending confirmation
_why: persistent notebook sessions for multi-step computation; API drift drops the lane

name: R Statistics MCP (rmcp)
extraction_hint: the 52 R statistical tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
_why: advanced R modeling lane; a tool-name change drops it to guidance-only

name: Monte Carlo Simulation MCP (MCS-MCP)
extraction_hint: simulation tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
_why: probabilistic modeling and revenue forecasting; an API change drops the lane

name: scikit-learn MCP (mcp-server-scikit-learn)
extraction_hint: ML classification/regression/clustering tool names; release API/slug pending confirmation
_why: ML prediction lane for audience segmentation and trend prediction; API drift drops it to guidance-only
